intake.user_request: Create a phased CODEREF-CORE workorder to consolidate split semantic systems. The main pipeline/scanner becomes the only production source of truth; semantic registry, source headers, and enrichment become projections from canonical ElementData.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Rule 4: goal mentions sequential gate keyword ('phase')

warnings: planner.discovery: docs/foundation/ absent; foundation-anchor enrichment skipped
Discovery candidates were narrowed from the broad keyword result to semantic scanner, populate, pipeline, scanner, type, and RAG surfaces that are directly relevant to this workorder.

in_scope: Make the canonical pipeline scanner the only production source of truth for semantic data.
Add CodeRef IDs to ElementData with configurable line-number anchoring.
Normalize paths to relative POSIX-style paths in .coderef outputs and CodeRef IDs.
Strengthen exports, imports, true reverse usedBy, related, and rules on enriched ElementData.
Generate semantic-registry.json as a projection from canonical ElementData.
Keep source headers optional, off by default, and projection-only.
Update populate CLI behavior, tests, and docs for the unified semantic flow.
out_of_scope: Replacing the full scanner architecture with a new scanner.
Making source headers the authoritative machine truth.

constraints: Source headers are optional human-facing projection output.
Production populate must not use src/semantic/ast-extractor.ts as a second scanner.
Existing RAG and graph consumers must continue to resolve CodeRef IDs.
